behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff: must prove both 401 response and zero enqueue calls. Unit is too low because a pure signature-check function cannot catch accidental forwarding. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess required (can run in-process), no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher level (System) because behavior text is about mismatch handling and forwarding logic, not real network or OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Start the HTTP handler in-process with a fake queue/repository module that records writes. Send a request object with invalid X-Signature-256 over a body. Assert HTTP status 401 and assert queue write method was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler + persistent queue adapter), so Unit is too low. Minimum infra is wired modules with controllable queue write completion; no real network/OS required. Rejected System: behavior text requires ordering relative to queue write, not real HTTP transport timing or kernel behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to the real queue module (or persistence-backed adapter) in a local test harness. Make queue write awaitable (e.g., deferred promise). Verify 200 is not emitted before write resolves, and is emitted after successful write.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is interaction between request path and async processor dispatch. Unit of a single function misses wiring mistakes where handler accidentally awaits processor completion. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no AI/ML; single-step flow.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with a downstream processor stub that blocks (never resolves or resolves late). Submit a valid event and assert response is returned promptly while processor is still pending.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency, which is sensitive to event loop scheduling, server runtime, and real HTTP request handling. In-process mocks can hide queueing/backpressure timing failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires real-time bound ('within 3 seconds wall-clock') under concurrent load.
behaviors.test_description: Start the actual Node.js service process and send concurrent real HTTP requests while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work. Measure end-to-end response times and assert each new request is acknowledged within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at module boundary (ID store/check + queue write). Unit tests of pure ID logic miss race/wiring mistakes between dedupe check and enqueue. Integration-brake: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; no AI/ML; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + dedupe store + queue modules together in-process. Send the same event ID twice. Assert first request writes once and second request does not create a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This verifies error propagation/mapping across queue module and HTTP layer. Unit is too low because it may miss adapter/handler contract mismatches. Rejected System because behavior text is specifically about translating queue 'full' signal to HTTP status/header, not real infrastructure timing/network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a queue module configured to return a deterministic 'full' error. Send a valid event and assert HTTP 503 plus Retry-After header value is present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Target is queue component behavior with real persistence wiring (capacity accounting + reject path). Unit may miss failures from repository/state interaction. System considered, but rejected because behavior text does not require real subprocess/HTTP/network; local persistence-backed module test is sufficient to expose real limit-enforcement bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against a local test datastore/filesystem backend. Configure small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more event. Assert overflow enqueue is rejected and persisted size/count never exceeds configured capacity.
